title: LFU Cache
questionBody:
Design and implement a data structure for a Least Frequently Used (LFU) cache.

Implement the LFUCache class:

  • LFUCache(int capacity) Initializes the object with the capacity of the data structure.
  • int get(int key) Gets the value of the key if the key exists in the cache. Otherwise, returns -1.
  • void put(int key, int value) Update the value of the key if present, or inserts the key if not already present. When the cache reaches its capacity, it should invalidate and remove the least frequently used key before inserting a new item. For this problem, when there is a tie (i.e., two or more keys with the same frequency), the least recently used key would be invalidated.

To determine the least frequently used key, a use counter is maintained for each key in the cache. The key with the smallest use counter is the least frequently used key.

When a key is first inserted into the cache, its use counter is set to 1 (due to the put operation). The use counter for a key in the cache is incremented either a get or put operation is called on it.

The functions get and put must each run in O(1) average time complexity.

 

Example 1:

Input
["LFUCache", "put", "put", "get", "put", "get", "get", "put", "get", "get", "get"]
[[2], [1, 1], [2, 2], [1], [3, 3], [2], [3], [4, 4], [1], [3], [4]]
Output
[null, null, null, 1, null, -1, 3, null, -1, 3, 4]

Explanation
// cnt(x) = the use counter for key x
// cache=[] will show the last used order for tiebreakers (leftmost element is  most recent)
LFUCache lfu = new LFUCache(2);
lfu.put(1, 1);   // cache=[1,_], cnt(1)=1
lfu.put(2, 2);   // cache=[2,1], cnt(2)=1, cnt(1)=1
lfu.get(1);      // return 1
                 // cache=[1,2], cnt(2)=1, cnt(1)=2
lfu.put(3, 3);   // 2 is the LFU key because cnt(2)=1 is the smallest, invalidate 2.
                 // cache=[3,1], cnt(3)=1, cnt(1)=2
lfu.get(2);      // return -1 (not found)
lfu.get(3);      // return 3
                 // cache=[3,1], cnt(3)=2, cnt(1)=2
lfu.put(4, 4);   // Both 1 and 3 have the same cnt, but 1 is LRU, invalidate 1.
                 // cache=[4,3], cnt(4)=1, cnt(3)=2
lfu.get(1);      // return -1 (not found)
lfu.get(3);      // return 3
                 // cache=[3,4], cnt(4)=1, cnt(3)=3
lfu.get(4);      // return 4
                 // cache=[4,3], cnt(4)=2, cnt(3)=3

 

Constraints:

  • 1 <= capacity <= 104
  • 0 <= key <= 105
  • 0 <= value <= 109
  • At most 2 * 105 calls will be made to get and put.
plainEnglishBody:
You are given an integer capacity representing the maximum number of keys the cache can hold.

Implement an LFU (Least Frequently Used) cache with get and put operations, adhering to the following rules: when a key is accessed or inserted, its frequency is incremented; when the cache is full and a new key is inserted, the least frequently used key is evicted; and if there is a tie in frequency, the least recently used key among those with the lowest frequency is evicted.

Specifically, implement the get(key) method, which returns the value of the key if present, otherwise returns -1, and increments the key's frequency if found.

Also, implement the put(key, value) method, which inserts the key if not already present, updates its value if present, and increments its frequency; if the cache is at full capacity, evict the LFU key before inserting.

examples.explanation: This example demonstrates the basic LFU eviction process. After putting (1,1) and (2,2), getting 1 increases its frequency. When putting (3,3), (2,2) gets evicted as it's the least frequently used. The following get operations showcase the behavior of cache hits and misses, including frequency updates.
With capacity 0, nothing can be stored in the cache, so every get operation will return -1.

This scenario highlights update functionality and eviction based on frequency and recency. Putting (2,3) updates the value. Then putting (4,1) evicts (1,1). Finally, get 1 returns -1 because it was evicted, and get 2 returns 3 because it was updated.

This test case shows how the cache can operate when it is not immediately full and eviction happens later. Initially, elements 1, 2, and 3 are inserted. After get operations, their frequency increments, so key 3 with the least frequency is evicted when (4,4) is added. Therefore get 3 returns -1 and get 4 returns 4.

This example illustrates the tie-breaking scenario. After putting 1 and getting 1, and putting 2 and getting 2 and getting 1. Then when putting 3, 2 gets evicted, since 2 and 1 both have freq 1 but 2 was added most recently. Following get operations help to clarify the state of the LFU cache after subsequent operations.

clarifyingQuestions: What are the possible ranges for the key and value, and can they be negative or zero?
What is the expected behavior if the capacity is zero or negative? Should I throw an exception or treat it as an empty cache?
If there are multiple keys with the same lowest frequency, how do I determine the least recently used key among them (specifically, what information do I need to store or track)?
If I call `get` with a non-existent key, should the frequency of the cache be updated in any way?
Can I assume that the capacity will always be an integer, and will I need to handle any potential memory limitations or overflow issues related to the cache size or frequency counters?

edgeCases.handling: All get and put operations should return -1 or do nothing respectively, as nothing can be stored.

edgeCases.edgeCase: Key already exists with the same value in put.
edgeCases.handling: Frequency should still be incremented, but no insertion should happen since the value is the same.

edgeCases.edgeCase: Putting a new key when the cache is full and multiple keys have the same minimum frequency.
edgeCases.handling: Evict the least recently used among the keys with the minimum frequency.

edgeCases.edgeCase: Getting a key that does not exist.
edgeCases.handling: Return -1 and do not modify frequencies.
Frequencies should be large enough to prevent overflow and potentially reset when approaching max values

edgeCases.edgeCase: Cache filled, LFU eviction required, min frequency is higher than 1 (e.g., 2 or 3).
edgeCases.handling: Correctly identify and evict keys with frequency equal to the current minimum frequency.

edgeCases.edgeCase: Repeatedly putting and getting the same key leading to a very high frequency
edgeCases.handling: The LFU cache needs to efficiently update the frequency list when a key's frequency becomes very high.

edgeCases.edgeCase: Extremely large number of unique keys are put in the cache.
edgeCases.handling: Data structures should support efficient addition and removal of keys without excessive memory overhead.

bruteForce.overview: The LFU cache stores key-value pairs and needs to evict the least frequently used one when it's full. The brute force approach to this problem involves keeping track of every single access and, when needed, going through all items to find the least used one.
steps: When a value is requested, record that it was requested.
When a new key-value pair needs to be added but the cache is full, examine the usage count of all currently stored key-value pairs.
Identify the key-value pair with the lowest usage count.
If multiple key-value pairs have the same lowest usage count, pick one of them to remove based on which was accessed the earliest.
Remove the selected key-value pair to make space for the new one.
Store the new key-value pair in the cache.

bigO.runtimeExplanation: The get operation has O(1) time complexity since we record access in constant time. When adding a new element and the cache is full, we need to iterate through all n items currently in the cache to determine the least frequently used item. This iteration to find the minimum frequency takes O(n) time. All other operations like adding or removing are O(1). Therefore, the overall complexity is dominated by the iteration to find the least frequent item.
bigO.spaceExplanation: The described solution stores key-value pairs along with their usage counts. In the worst-case scenario, the cache could hold N key-value pairs, where N is the maximum capacity of the cache. Therefore, we need to maintain auxiliary space proportional to the number of items in the cache for storing the key-value pairs and their associated usage counts. This leads to a space complexity of O(N).

class LFUCache:

    def __init__(self, capacity: int):
        self.capacity = capacity
        self.cache = {}
        self.usage_count = {}
        self.access_order = {}
        self.current_time = 0

    def get(self, key: int) -> int:
        if key in self.cache:
            self.usage_count[key] += 1
            self.access_order[key] = self.current_time
            self.current_time += 1
            return self.cache[key]
        return -1

    def put(self, key: int, value: int) -> None:
        self.current_time += 1
        if self.capacity == 0:
            return

        if key in self.cache:
            self.cache[key] = value
            self.usage_count[key] += 1
            self.access_order[key] = self.current_time
            return

        if len(self.cache) == self.capacity:
            # Need to evict the least frequently used item
            least_frequent = float('inf')
            key_to_evict = None

            for cache_key, frequency in self.usage_count.items():
                if frequency < least_frequent and cache_key in self.cache:
                    least_frequent = frequency
                    key_to_evict = cache_key
                elif frequency == least_frequent and cache_key in self.cache:
                    # Break ties based on access order
                    if self.access_order[cache_key] < self.access_order[key_to_evict]:
                        key_to_evict = cache_key

            del self.cache[key_to_evict]
            del self.usage_count[key_to_evict]
            del self.access_order[key_to_evict]

        self.cache[key] = value
        self.usage_count[key] = 1
        self.access_order[key] = self.current_time

class LFUCache {

  constructor(capacity) {
    this.capacity = capacity;
    this.cache = new Map();
    this.usageCounts = new Map();
    this.accessTimestamps = new Map();
  }

  get(key) {
    if (!this.cache.has(key)) {
      return -1;
    }

    this.usageCounts.set(key, this.usageCounts.get(key) + 1);
    this.accessTimestamps.set(key, Date.now());
    return this.cache.get(key);
  }

  put(key, value) {
    if (this.capacity <= 0) {
      return;
    }

    if (this.cache.has(key)) {
      this.cache.set(key, value);
      this.usageCounts.set(key, this.usageCounts.get(key) + 1);
      this.accessTimestamps.set(key, Date.now());
      return;
    }

    if (this.cache.size >= this.capacity) {
      // Cache is full, evict least frequently used
      let keyToEvict = null;
      let minUsageCount = Infinity;
      let earliestTimestamp = Infinity;

      for (const [cachedKey, usageCount] of this.usageCounts) {
        if (usageCount < minUsageCount) {
          minUsageCount = usageCount;
          keyToEvict = cachedKey;
          earliestTimestamp = this.accessTimestamps.get(cachedKey);
        } else if (usageCount === minUsageCount) {
          // If usage counts are equal, check access time
          const currentTimestamp = this.accessTimestamps.get(cachedKey);
          if (currentTimestamp < earliestTimestamp) {
            earliestTimestamp = currentTimestamp;
            keyToEvict = cachedKey;
          }
        }
      }

      // Evict the least frequently used key
      this.cache.delete(keyToEvict);
      this.usageCounts.delete(keyToEvict);
      this.accessTimestamps.delete(keyToEvict);
    }

    this.cache.set(key, value);
    this.usageCounts.set(key, 1);
    this.accessTimestamps.set(key, Date.now());
  }
}

TypeScript.html:
class LFUCache {

    private cache: Map<number, any>;
    private usageCounts: Map<number, number>;
    private lastAccessTimes: Map<number, number>;
    private capacity: number;
    private currentTime: number;

    constructor(capacity: number) {
        this.cache = new Map();
        this.usageCounts = new Map();
        this.lastAccessTimes = new Map();
        this.capacity = capacity;
        this.currentTime = 0;
    }

    get(key: number): any {
        if (!this.cache.has(key)) {
            return -1;
        }

        this.usageCounts.set(key, (this.usageCounts.get(key) || 0) + 1);
        this.lastAccessTimes.set(key, this.currentTime++);
        return this.cache.get(key);
    }

    put(key: number, value: any): void {
        if (this.capacity <= 0) {
            return;
        }

        if (this.cache.has(key)) {
            this.cache.set(key, value);
            this.usageCounts.set(key, (this.usageCounts.get(key) || 0) + 1);
            this.lastAccessTimes.set(key, this.currentTime++);
            return;
        }

        if (this.cache.size >= this.capacity) {
            // Evict the least frequently used item

            let leastFrequentKey: number | null = null;
            let minUsageCount = Infinity;
            let oldestAccessTime = Infinity;

            for (const [currentKey, usageCount] of this.usageCounts) {
                if (usageCount < minUsageCount) {
                    minUsageCount = usageCount;
                    leastFrequentKey = currentKey;
                    oldestAccessTime = this.lastAccessTimes.get(currentKey) || 0;
                } else if (usageCount === minUsageCount) {
                    // Prioritize based on the oldest access time

                    if ((this.lastAccessTimes.get(currentKey) || 0) < oldestAccessTime) {
                        oldestAccessTime = this.lastAccessTimes.get(currentKey) || 0;
                        leastFrequentKey = currentKey;
                    }
                }
            }

            // Remove the least frequently used entry

            if (leastFrequentKey !== null) {
                this.cache.delete(leastFrequentKey);
                this.usageCounts.delete(leastFrequentKey);
                this.lastAccessTimes.delete(leastFrequentKey);
            }
        }

        this.cache.set(key, value);
        this.usageCounts.set(key, 1);
        this.lastAccessTimes.set(key, this.currentTime++);
    }
}

Java.html:
class LFUCache {

    private int capacity;
    private Map<Integer, Integer> cache;
    private Map<Integer, Integer> accessCounts;
    private Map<Integer, Long> accessTimestamps;
    private long timestamp;

    public LFUCache(int capacity) {
        this.capacity = capacity;
        this.cache = new HashMap<>();
        this.accessCounts = new HashMap<>();
        this.accessTimestamps = new HashMap<>();
        this.timestamp = 0;
    }

    public int get(int key) {
        if (!cache.containsKey(key)) {
            return -1;
        }

        // Increment access count and update timestamp
        accessCounts.put(key, accessCounts.get(key) + 1);
        accessTimestamps.put(key, timestamp++);

        return cache.get(key);
    }

    public void put(int key, int value) {
        if (capacity <= 0) {
            return;
        }

        if (cache.containsKey(key)) {
            cache.put(key, value);
            accessCounts.put(key, accessCounts.get(key) + 1);
            accessTimestamps.put(key, timestamp++);
            return;
        }

        if (cache.size() == capacity) {
            // Evict the least frequently used item
            int keyToEvict = findKeyToEvict();
            cache.remove(keyToEvict);
            accessCounts.remove(keyToEvict);
            accessTimestamps.remove(keyToEvict);
        }

        cache.put(key, value);
        accessCounts.put(key, 1);
        accessTimestamps.put(key, timestamp++);
    }

    private int findKeyToEvict() {
        int minAccessCount = Integer.MAX_VALUE;
        int keyToEvict = -1;
        long earliestTimestamp = Long.MAX_VALUE;

        for (int key : cache.keySet()) {
            int currentAccessCount = accessCounts.get(key);

            //Find min access count to identify candidates
            if (currentAccessCount < minAccessCount) {
                minAccessCount = currentAccessCount;
                keyToEvict = key;
                earliestTimestamp = accessTimestamps.get(key);

            } else if (currentAccessCount == minAccessCount) {
                // If same count, evict based on timestamp
                if (accessTimestamps.get(key) < earliestTimestamp) {
                    earliestTimestamp = accessTimestamps.get(key);
                    keyToEvict = key;
                }
            }
        }

        return keyToEvict;
    }
}

C++.html:
class LFUCache {
public:
    LFUCache(int capacity) : cacheCapacity(capacity) {}

    int get(int key) {
        if (cacheMap.find(key) == cacheMap.end()) {
            return -1;
        }

        accessCount[key]++;
        accessOrder[key]++;
        return cacheMap[key];
    }

    void put(int key, int value) {
        if (cacheCapacity <= 0) return;

        if (cacheMap.find(key) != cacheMap.end()) {
            cacheMap[key] = value;
            accessCount[key]++;
            accessOrder[key]++;
            return;
        }

        if (cacheMap.size() == cacheCapacity) {
            // Need to evict the least frequently used element

            int keyToEvict = -1;
            int minFrequency = INT_MAX;
            int earliestAccess = INT_MAX;

            for (auto const& [cacheKey, cacheValue] : cacheMap) {
                if (accessCount[cacheKey] < minFrequency) {
                    minFrequency = accessCount[cacheKey];
                    keyToEvict = cacheKey;
                    earliestAccess = accessOrder[cacheKey];
                } else if (accessCount[cacheKey] == minFrequency && accessOrder[cacheKey] < earliestAccess) {
                    // Break ties with oldest access
                    keyToEvict = cacheKey;
                    earliestAccess = accessOrder[cacheKey];
                }
            }

            cacheMap.erase(keyToEvict);
            accessCount.erase(keyToEvict);
            accessOrder.erase(keyToEvict);
        }

        cacheMap[key] = value;
        accessCount[key] = 1;
        accessOrder[key] = nextAccessOrder++;
    }

private:
    int cacheCapacity;
    unordered_map<int, int> cacheMap;
    unordered_map<int, int> accessCount;
    unordered_map<int, int> accessOrder;
    int nextAccessOrder = 0;
};

C#.html:
public class LFUCache
{

    private class CacheItem
    {
        public int Key { get; set; }
        public int Value { get; set; }
        public int UsageCount { get; set; }
        public DateTime LastAccessed { get; set; }
    }

    private readonly int capacity;
    private readonly List<CacheItem> cache;

    public LFUCache(int capacity)
    {
        this.capacity = capacity;
        this.cache = new List<CacheItem>();
    }

    public int Get(int key)
    {
        CacheItem existingItem = cache.FirstOrDefault(item => item.Key == key);

        if (existingItem != null)
        {
            existingItem.UsageCount++;
            existingItem.LastAccessed = DateTime.Now;
            return existingItem.Value;
        }

        return -1;
    }

    public void Put(int key, int value)
    {
        if (capacity <= 0)
        {
            return;
        }

        CacheItem existingItem = cache.FirstOrDefault(item => item.Key == key);

        if (existingItem != null)
        {
            existingItem.Value = value;
            existingItem.UsageCount++;
            existingItem.LastAccessed = DateTime.Now;
            return;
        }

        if (cache.Count == capacity)
        {
            // Cache is full, need to evict an item.

            CacheItem leastFrequentlyUsed = null;
            int minUsageCount = int.MaxValue;

            foreach (var item in cache)
            {
                if (item.UsageCount < minUsageCount)
                {
                    minUsageCount = item.UsageCount;
                    leastFrequentlyUsed = item;
                }
                else if (item.UsageCount == minUsageCount)
                {
                    // Tie breaker, choose the oldest.
                    if (item.LastAccessed < leastFrequentlyUsed.LastAccessed)
                    {
                        leastFrequentlyUsed = item;
                    }
                }
            }

            // Remove the least frequently used item.
            cache.Remove(leastFrequentlyUsed);
        }

        // Add the new item to the cache.
        CacheItem newItem = new CacheItem
        {
            Key = key,
            Value = value,
            UsageCount = 1,
            LastAccessed = DateTime.Now
        };

        cache.Add(newItem);
    }
}

Go.html:
package main

type lfuCache struct {
	capacity int
	cache map[int]int
	usageCount map[int]int
	accessOrder []int
}

func Constructor(capacity int) lfuCache {
	return lfuCache{
		capacity:    capacity,
		cache:       make(map[int]int),
		usageCount:  make(map[int]int),
		accessOrder: make([]int, 0),
	}
}

func (this *lfuCache) Get(key int) int {
	if _, ok := this.cache[key]; !ok {
		return -1
	}

	this.usageCount[key]++

	// Update access order to reflect recent use.
	this.updateAccessOrder(key)

	return this.cache[key]
}

func (this *lfuCache) Put(key int, value int) {
	if this.capacity <= 0 {
		return
	}

	if _, ok := this.cache[key]; ok {
		this.cache[key] = value
		this.usageCount[key]++
		this.updateAccessOrder(key)
		return
	}

	if len(this.cache) >= this.capacity {
		// Evict the least frequently used key
		leastFrequentKey := this.findLeastFrequentKey()
		delete(this.cache, leastFrequentKey)
		delete(this.usageCount, leastFrequentKey)

		// Remove from access order.
		this.removeFromAccessOrder(leastFrequentKey)
	}

	this.cache[key] = value
	this.usageCount[key] = 1
	this.accessOrder = append(this.accessOrder, key)
}

func (this *lfuCache) findLeastFrequentKey() int {
	leastFrequentKey := this.accessOrder[0]
	minUsageCount := this.usageCount[leastFrequentKey]

	for _, key := range this.accessOrder {
		if this.usageCount[key] < minUsageCount {
			minUsageCount = this.usageCount[key]
			leastFrequentKey = key
		}
	}

	return leastFrequentKey
}

func (this *lfuCache) updateAccessOrder(key int) {
	// Move the accessed key to the end of access order.
	this.removeFromAccessOrder(key)
	this.accessOrder = append(this.accessOrder, key)
}

func (this *lfuCache) removeFromAccessOrder(key int) {
	for i, k := range this.accessOrder {
		if k == key {
			this.accessOrder = append(this.accessOrder[:i], this.accessOrder[i+1:]...)
			return
		}
	}
}

Rust.html:
use std::collections::HashMap;

struct LfuCache {
    capacity: usize,
    cache: HashMap<i32, i32>,
    usage_count: HashMap<i32, usize>,
    access_order: Vec<i32>,
}

impl LfuCache {
    fn new(capacity: i32) -> Self {
        LfuCache {
            capacity: capacity as usize,
            cache: HashMap::new(),
            usage_count: HashMap::new(),
            access_order: Vec::new(),
        }
    }

    fn get(&mut self, key: i32) -> i32 {
        if let Some(&value) = self.cache.get(&key) {
            // Update usage count when the key is accessed
            if let Some(count) = self.usage_count.get_mut(&key) {
                *count += 1;
            }

            if let Some(index) = self.access_order.iter().position(|&x| x == key) {
                self.access_order.remove(index);
                self.access_order.push(key);
            }

            value
        } else {
            -1
        }
    }

    fn put(&mut self, key: i32, value: i32) {
        if self.capacity == 0 {
            return;
        }

        if self.cache.contains_key(&key) {
            self.cache.insert(key, value);
            if let Some(count) = self.usage_count.get_mut(&key) {
                *count += 1;
            }
            if let Some(index) = self.access_order.iter().position(|&x| x == key) {
                self.access_order.remove(index);
                self.access_order.push(key);
            }

            return;
        }

        if self.cache.len() == self.capacity {
            // Evict the least frequently used key
            let mut least_frequent_key: Option<i32> = None;
            let mut min_usage = usize::MAX;

            for &cached_key in &self.access_order {
                if let Some(&usage) = self.usage_count.get(&cached_key) {
                    if usage < min_usage {
                        min_usage = usage;
                        least_frequent_key = Some(cached_key);
                    }
                }
            }

            // Remove the least frequently used key
            if let Some(key_to_remove) = least_frequent_key {
                self.cache.remove(&key_to_remove);
                self.usage_count.remove(&key_to_remove);

                if let Some(index) = self.access_order.iter().position(|&x| x == key_to_remove) {
                    self.access_order.remove(index);
                }
            }
        }

        self.cache.insert(key, value);
        self.usage_count.insert(key, 1);
        self.access_order.push(key);
    }
}

Kotlin.html:
class LFUCache(
    private val capacity: Int
) {

    private val cache = mutableMapOf<Int, Int>()
    private val accessCounts = mutableMapOf<Int, Int>()
    private val accessOrder = mutableListOf<Int>()

    fun get(key: Int): Int {
        if (cache.containsKey(key)) {
            accessCounts[key] = accessCounts.getOrDefault(key, 0) + 1
            accessOrder.remove(key)
            accessOrder.add(key)
            return cache[key]!!
        } else {
            return -1
        }
    }

    fun put(key: Int, value: Int) {
        if (capacity <= 0) return

        if (cache.containsKey(key)) {
            cache[key] = value
            accessCounts[key] = accessCounts.getOrDefault(key, 0) + 1
            accessOrder.remove(key)
            accessOrder.add(key)
            return
        }

        if (cache.size == capacity) {
            // Need to evict the least frequently used element
            var keyToEvict: Int? = null
            var minCount = Int.MAX_VALUE

            for (cachedKey in cache.keys) {
                val count = accessCounts.getOrDefault(cachedKey, 0)
                if (count < minCount) {
                    minCount = count
                    keyToEvict = cachedKey
                } else if (count == minCount) {
                    // Break ties based on access order
                    val currentCandidateIndex =
                        accessOrder.indexOf(keyToEvict)
                    val newCandidateIndex = accessOrder.indexOf(cachedKey)
                    if (newCandidateIndex < currentCandidateIndex) {
                        keyToEvict = cachedKey
                    }
                }
            }

            // Remove the least used item
            if (keyToEvict != null) {
                cache.remove(keyToEvict)
                accessCounts.remove(keyToEvict)
                accessOrder.remove(keyToEvict)
            }
        }

        // Add the new key-value pair to the cache
        cache[key] = value
        accessCounts[key] = 1

        // Update access order to reflect the new entry
        accessOrder.add(key)
    }

}

class LFUCache {

    private var cache: [Int: Int]
    private var usageCounts: [Int: Int]
    private var accessOrder: [Int]
    private let capacity: Int

    init(_ capacity: Int) {
        self.capacity = capacity
        self.cache = [:]
        self.usageCounts = [:]
        self.accessOrder = []
    }

    func get(_ key: Int) -> Int {
        if let value = cache[key] {
            usageCounts[key, default: 0] += 1
            accessOrder.append(key)
            return value
        } else {
            return -1
        }
    }

    func put(_ key: Int, _ value: Int) {
        guard capacity > 0 else { return }

        if cache[key] != nil {
            cache[key] = value
            usageCounts[key, default: 0] += 1
            accessOrder.append(key)
            return
        }

        if cache.count == capacity {
            // Need to evict least frequently used
            let keyToRemove = findLeastFrequentlyUsed()

            cache.removeValue(forKey: keyToRemove)
            usageCounts.removeValue(forKey: keyToRemove)
        }

        cache[key] = value
        usageCounts[key] = 1
        accessOrder.append(key)
    }

    private func findLeastFrequentlyUsed() -> Int {
        guard !usageCounts.isEmpty else { return -1 }

        var minFrequency = Int.max
        var leastFrequentKeys: [Int] = []

        // Find the minimum frequency
        for (key, frequency) in usageCounts {
            if frequency < minFrequency {
                minFrequency = frequency
                leastFrequentKeys = [key]
            } else if frequency == minFrequency {
                leastFrequentKeys.append(key)
            }
        }

        //If only one key has the min frequency
        if leastFrequentKeys.count == 1 {
            return leastFrequentKeys[0]
        }

        //Find the least recently used key with the min frequency
        var keyToRemove = -1
        var oldestIndex = Int.max
        for key in leastFrequentKeys {
            if let index = accessOrder.firstIndex(of: key) {
                if index < oldestIndex {
                    oldestIndex = index
                    keyToRemove = key
                }
            }
        }

        //This section is for cleaning the accessOrder array.
        accessOrder = accessOrder.filter { !leastFrequentKeys.contains($0) }
        return keyToRemove
    }
}

class LFUCache

def initialize(capacity)
	@capacity = capacity
	@cache = {}
	@access_counts = {}
	@access_order = []
end

def get(key)
	if @cache.key?(key)
		@access_counts[key] += 1
		@access_order.delete(key)
		@access_order << key
		@cache[key]
	else
		nil
	end
end

def put(key, value)
	if @capacity <= 0
		return
	end

	if @cache.key?(key)
		@cache[key] = value
		@access_counts[key] += 1
		@access_order.delete(key)
		@access_order << key
		return
	end

	if @cache.length >= @capacity
		# Need to evict the least frequently used
		least_frequent_key = find_least_frequent_key
		@cache.delete(least_frequent_key)
		@access_counts.delete(least_frequent_key)
		@access_order.delete(least_frequent_key)
	end

	@cache[key] = value
	@access_counts[key] = 1
	@access_order << key
end

private

def find_least_frequent_key
	# Find the key with the lowest access count, tiebreaker is access order
	min_count = Float::INFINITY
	least_frequent_key = nil

	@access_counts.each do |key, count|
		if count < min_count
			min_count = count
			least_frequent_key = key
		end
	end

	# Handle ties based on access order
	ties = @access_counts.select { |key, count| count == min_count }.keys

	if ties.length > 1
		# Need to consider the order they were accessed in
		index_of_least_used = @access_order.index(ties[0])
		least_frequent_key = ties[0]
		ties.each do |possible_key|
			current_index = @access_order.index(possible_key)
			if current_index < index_of_least_used
				index_of_least_used = current_index
				least_frequent_key = possible_key
			end
		end
	end

	least_frequent_key
end
end

optimal.overview: We want to create a special storage system that quickly finds and removes items used least often. We'll track how frequently each item is used, and when we run out of space, we'll remove the least frequently used one.
steps: Keep track of two things for each item: its value and how many times it has been used.
Also, keep a record of the different usage counts. For example, we need to know which items have been used once, which have been used twice, and so on.
When we need to get an item, we look it up and increase its usage count. Then we move it to the correct group based on its new, higher usage count.
When we need to add a new item and the storage is full, we find the group of items with the lowest usage count.
From that group of items with the lowest usage count, we remove the item that was added to the storage system the earliest.
Then, we add the new item to the group with a usage count of one.
By keeping items grouped by usage count and tracking the order they were added, we can quickly find the least frequently used item when we need to make space.

bigO.runtimeExplanation: The LFU cache operations described, get and put, rely on hash maps (dictionaries) and doubly linked lists. Hash map lookups and insertions are O(1) on average. Moving elements between frequency lists in the doubly linked list is also O(1). Therefore, the time complexity of both get and put operations is O(1), assuming hash collisions are minimal. Note that 'n' in this context would represent the cache capacity, but the operations are designed to be independent of the cache size.
bigO.spaceExplanation: The solution uses a hash map to store the value and usage count for each item, which can grow up to the cache's capacity N. It also maintains a hash map to group items by their usage counts; in the worst case, each item has a unique usage count, leading to N entries. Therefore, the auxiliary space used is proportional to the cache's capacity, N, resulting in a space complexity of O(N).

class LFUCache:

def __init__(self, capacity):
        self.capacity = capacity
        self.cache = {}
        self.frequencyMap = {}
        self.frequencyListHeads = {}
        self.leastFrequency = 1

def get(self, key):
        if key not in self.cache:
            return -1
        
        node = self.cache[key]
        self._update_frequency(node)
        return node.value

def put(self, key, value):
        if self.capacity == 0:
            return

        if key in self.cache:
            node = self.cache[key]
            node.value = value
            self._update_frequency(node)
            return

        if len(self.cache) == self.capacity:
            # Remove the least frequently used item when cache is full.
            frequencyList = self.frequencyListHeads[self.leastFrequency]
            removedNode = frequencyList.remove_tail()
            del self.cache[removedNode.key]

        newNode = Node(key, value)
        self.cache[key] = newNode
        self.leastFrequency = 1

        if 1 not in self.frequencyListHeads:
            self.frequencyListHeads[1] = DoublyLinkedList()

        self.frequencyListHeads[1].add_node(newNode)
        self.frequencyMap[key] = 1

def _update_frequency(self, node):
        key = node.key
        currentFrequency = self.frequencyMap[key]
        newFrequency = currentFrequency + 1

        # Remove the node from its current frequency list.
        self.frequencyListHeads[currentFrequency].remove_node(node)
        if self.leastFrequency == currentFrequency and self.frequencyListHeads[currentFrequency].size == 0:
            self.leastFrequency += 1

        # Add the node to the new frequency list.
        if newFrequency not in self.frequencyListHeads:
            self.frequencyListHeads[newFrequency] = DoublyLinkedList()

        self.frequencyListHeads[newFrequency].add_node(node)
        self.frequencyMap[key] = newFrequency

class Node:
    def __init__(self, key, value):
        self.key = key
        self.value = value
        self.frequency = 0
        self.next = None
        self.prev = None

class DoublyLinkedList:
    def __init__(self):
        self.head = Node(0, 0)
        self.tail = Node(0, 0)
        self.head.next = self.tail
        self.tail.prev = self.head
        self.size = 0

    def add_node(self, node):
        # Add the node right after head.
        node.next = self.head.next
        node.prev = self.head
        self.head.next.prev = node
        self.head.next = node
        self.size += 1

    def remove_node(self, node):
        # Remove the node from the list.
        node.prev.next = node.next
        node.next.prev = node.prev
        self.size -= 1

    def remove_tail(self):
        # Remove the tail node, which is the LRU node for the frequency.
        tailNode = self.tail.prev
        self.remove_node(tailNode)
        return tailNode

class Node {
  constructor(key, value) {
    this.key = key;
    this.value = value;
    this.frequency = 1;
  }
}

class LFUCache {
  constructor(capacity) {
    this.capacity = capacity;
    this.cache = new Map();
    this.frequencyMap = new Map();
    this.minFrequency = 0;
  }

  get(key) {
    if (!this.cache.has(key)) {
      return -1;
    }

    const node = this.cache.get(key);
    this.updateFrequency(node);
    return node.value;
  }

  put(key, value) {
    if (this.capacity <= 0) {
      return;
    }

    if (this.cache.has(key)) {
      const node = this.cache.get(key);
      node.value = value;
      this.updateFrequency(node);
      return;
    }

    if (this.cache.size === this.capacity) {
      this.evictLeastFrequentlyUsed();
    }

    const node = new Node(key, value);
    this.cache.set(key, node);

    // Initialize frequency for new node.
    this.minFrequency = 1;
    if (!this.frequencyMap.has(1)) {
      this.frequencyMap.set(1, new Map());
    }
    this.frequencyMap.get(1).set(key, node);
  }

  updateFrequency(node) {
    const oldFrequency = node.frequency;
    let frequencyList = this.frequencyMap.get(oldFrequency);
    frequencyList.delete(node.key);

    // Clean up empty frequency list.
    if (frequencyList.size === 0) {
      this.frequencyMap.delete(oldFrequency);
      if (oldFrequency === this.minFrequency) {
        this.minFrequency++;
      }
    }

    node.frequency++;
    const newFrequency = node.frequency;

    if (!this.frequencyMap.has(newFrequency)) {
      this.frequencyMap.set(newFrequency, new Map());
    }
    this.frequencyMap.get(newFrequency).set(node.key, node);
  }

  evictLeastFrequentlyUsed() {
    // Must evict from the lowest frequency.
    const frequencyList = this.frequencyMap.get(this.minFrequency);

    // Get the first key in the map to evict.
    const keyToEvict = frequencyList.keys().next().value;

    frequencyList.delete(keyToEvict);
    if (frequencyList.size === 0) {
      this.frequencyMap.delete(this.minFrequency);
    }

    this.cache.delete(keyToEvict);
  }
}

class LFUCache {
  private readonly capacity: number;
  private readonly cache: Map<number, number>;
  private readonly frequencyMap: Map<number, Set<number>>;
  private readonly keyFrequencyMap: Map<number, number>;
  private minFrequency: number;

  constructor(capacity: number) {
    this.capacity = capacity;
    this.cache = new Map();
    this.frequencyMap = new Map();
    this.keyFrequencyMap = new Map();
    this.minFrequency = 1;
  }

  get(key: number): number {
    if (!this.cache.has(key)) {
      return -1;
    }

    this.updateFrequency(key);
    return this.cache.get(key)!;
  }

  put(key: number, value: number): void {
    if (this.capacity <= 0) {
      return;
    }

    if (this.cache.has(key)) {
      this.cache.set(key, value);
      this.updateFrequency(key);
      return;
    }

    if (this.cache.size >= this.capacity) {
      // Remove the least frequently used item
      const keysWithMinFrequency = this.frequencyMap.get(this.minFrequency)!;
      const keyToDelete = keysWithMinFrequency.values().next().value;
      keysWithMinFrequency.delete(keyToDelete);

      if (keysWithMinFrequency.size === 0) {
        this.frequencyMap.delete(this.minFrequency);
      }

      this.cache.delete(keyToDelete);
      this.keyFrequencyMap.delete(keyToDelete);
    }

    this.cache.set(key, value);
    this.keyFrequencyMap.set(key, 1);

    // New keys start with frequency 1
    if (!this.frequencyMap.has(1)) {
      this.frequencyMap.set(1, new Set());
    }

    this.frequencyMap.get(1)!.add(key);
    this.minFrequency = 1;
  }

  private updateFrequency(key: number): void {
    const oldFrequency = this.keyFrequencyMap.get(key)!;
    this.keyFrequencyMap.set(key, oldFrequency + 1);

    // Remove key from old frequency set
    this.frequencyMap.get(oldFrequency)!.delete(key);

    if (this.frequencyMap.get(oldFrequency)!.size === 0) {
      this.frequencyMap.delete(oldFrequency);

      // Update minFrequency if needed
      if (this.minFrequency === oldFrequency) {
        this.minFrequency++;
      }
    }

    const newFrequency = oldFrequency + 1;

    // Add key to new frequency set
    if (!this.frequencyMap.has(newFrequency)) {
      this.frequencyMap.set(newFrequency, new Set());
    }

    this.frequencyMap.get(newFrequency)!.add(key);
  }
}

import java.util.HashMap;
import java.util.LinkedHashSet;

class LFUCache {

    private HashMap<Integer, Integer> keyToValueMap;
    private HashMap<Integer, Integer> keyToFrequencyMap;
    private HashMap<Integer, LinkedHashSet<Integer>> frequencyToKeysMap;
    private int capacity;
    private int minimumFrequency;

    public LFUCache(int capacity) {
        this.capacity = capacity;
        keyToValueMap = new HashMap<>();
        keyToFrequencyMap = new HashMap<>();
        frequencyToKeysMap = new HashMap<>();
        minimumFrequency = 0;
    }

    public int get(int key) {
        if (!keyToValueMap.containsKey(key)) {
            return -1;
        }

        updateFrequency(key);
        return keyToValueMap.get(key);
    }

    public void put(int key, int value) {
        if (capacity <= 0) {
            return;
        }

        if (keyToValueMap.containsKey(key)) {
            keyToValueMap.put(key, value);
            updateFrequency(key);
            return;
        }

        if (keyToValueMap.size() == capacity) {
            // Need to evict the least frequently used item.
            evictLeastFrequent();
        }

        keyToValueMap.put(key, value);
        keyToFrequencyMap.put(key, 1);
        // Initialize the frequency of the new key.
        frequencyToKeysMap.computeIfAbsent(1, k -> new LinkedHashSet<>()).add(key);
        minimumFrequency = 1;
    }

    private void updateFrequency(int key) {
        int oldFrequency = keyToFrequencyMap.get(key);
        keyToFrequencyMap.put(key, oldFrequency + 1);
        frequencyToKeysMap.get(oldFrequency).remove(key);

        // If the last key with the min frequency was removed,
        // update the minimum frequency.
        if (oldFrequency == minimumFrequency && frequencyToKeysMap.get(oldFrequency).isEmpty()) {
            minimumFrequency++;
        }

        // Move the key to the next higher frequency.
        frequencyToKeysMap.computeIfAbsent(oldFrequency + 1, k -> new LinkedHashSet<>()).add(key);
    }

    private void evictLeastFrequent() {
        // Get the key to delete from the least frequent bucket.
        int keyToDelete = frequencyToKeysMap.get(minimumFrequency).iterator().next();

        // Because we need to find the LFU.
        frequencyToKeysMap.get(minimumFrequency).remove(keyToDelete);
        keyToValueMap.remove(keyToDelete);
        keyToFrequencyMap.remove(keyToDelete);
    }
}

#include <iostream>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <list>

class LFUCache {
public:
    LFUCache(int capacityValue) : capacity(capacityValue), leastFrequency(1) {}

    int get(int key) {
        if (keyToValueMap.find(key) == keyToValueMap.end()) {
            return -1;
        }

        updateFrequency(key);
        return keyToValueMap[key];
    }

    void put(int key, int value) {
        if (capacity <= 0) return;

        if (keyToValueMap.find(key) != keyToValueMap.end()) {
            keyToValueMap[key] = value;
            updateFrequency(key);
            return;
        }

        if (keyToValueMap.size() == capacity) {
            // Need to evict the least frequently used key
            int keyToRemove = frequencyToKeysMap[leastFrequency].front();
            frequencyToKeysMap[leastFrequency].pop_front();

            keyToValueMap.erase(keyToRemove);
            keyToFrequencyMap.erase(keyToRemove);

            if (frequencyToKeysMap[leastFrequency].empty()) {
                frequencyToKeysMap.erase(leastFrequency);
            }
        }

        keyToValueMap[key] = value;
        keyToFrequencyMap[key] = 1;

        // Adding a new key, so its frequency is 1
        frequencyToKeysMap[1].push_back(key);
        leastFrequency = 1;
    }

private:
    int capacity;
    int leastFrequency;
    std::unordered_map<int, int> keyToValueMap;
    std::unordered_map<int, int> keyToFrequencyMap;
    std::unordered_map<int, std::list<int>> frequencyToKeysMap;

    void updateFrequency(int key) {
        int currentFrequency = keyToFrequencyMap[key];
        keyToFrequencyMap[key] = currentFrequency + 1;

        // Removing key from current frequency list
        frequencyToKeysMap[currentFrequency].remove(key);

        if (frequencyToKeysMap[currentFrequency].empty()) {

            // Current frequency list is empty.
            frequencyToKeysMap.erase(currentFrequency);

            // Update leastFrequency if needed
            if (leastFrequency == currentFrequency) {
                leastFrequency++;
            }
        }

        // Add key to the next frequency list
        frequencyToKeysMap[currentFrequency + 1].push_back(key);
    }
};

            return;
        }

        if (_valueMap.ContainsKey(key))
        {
            _valueMap[key] = value;
            UpdateFrequency(key);
            return;
        }

        if (_valueMap.Count >= _capacity)
        {
            // Evict the LFU item before adding new item
            EvictLeastFrequentlyUsed();
        }

        _valueMap[key] = value;
        _frequencyMap[key] = 1;

        // When new item is added, frequency is 1
        if (!_frequencyListMap.ContainsKey(1))
        {
            _frequencyListMap[1] = new LinkedList<int>();
        }

        LinkedListNode<int> newNode = new LinkedListNode<int>(key);
        _frequencyListMap[1].AddLast(newNode);
        _nodeMap[key] = newNode;
        _minimumFrequency = 1;
    }

    private void UpdateFrequency(int key)
    {
        int oldFrequency = _frequencyMap[key];
        int newFrequency = oldFrequency + 1;
        _frequencyMap[key] = newFrequency;

        // Move the key to the new frequency list
        _frequencyListMap[oldFrequency].Remove(_nodeMap[key]);

        // Remove empty list to save space
        if (_frequencyListMap[oldFrequency].Count == 0)
        {
            _frequencyListMap.Remove(oldFrequency);
            if (_minimumFrequency == oldFrequency)
            {
                _minimumFrequency++;
            }
        }

        if (!_frequencyListMap.ContainsKey(newFrequency))
        {
            _frequencyListMap[newFrequency] = new LinkedList<int>();
        }

        LinkedListNode<int> newNode = new LinkedListNode<int>(key);
        _frequencyListMap[newFrequency].AddLast(newNode);
        _nodeMap[key] = newNode;
    }

    private void EvictLeastFrequentlyUsed()
    {
        // Remove the least frequently used item.
        LinkedList<int> listToEvictFrom = _frequencyListMap[_minimumFrequency];
        int keyToEvict = listToEvictFrom.First.Value;

        listToEvictFrom.RemoveFirst();

        _valueMap.Remove(keyToEvict);
        _frequencyMap.Remove(keyToEvict);
        _nodeMap.Remove(keyToEvict);

        // If frequency is empty, remove it
        if (listToEvictFrom.Count == 0)
        {
            _frequencyListMap.Remove(_minimumFrequency);
        }
    }
}

package main

type LFUCache struct {
	capacity            int
	cache               map[int]*node
	frequencyMap        map[int]*doublyLinkedList
	minimumFrequency    int
}

type node struct {
	key, value, frequency int
	prev, next            *node
}

type doublyLinkedList struct {
	head, tail *node
}

func newDoublyLinkedList() *doublyLinkedList {
	head := &node{}
	tail := &node{}
	head.next = tail
	tail.prev = head
	return &doublyLinkedList{head: head, tail: tail}
}

func (dll *doublyLinkedList) addNode(node *node) {
	node.next = dll.head.next
	node.prev = dll.head
	dll.head.next.prev = node
	dll.head.next = node
}

func (dll *doublyLinkedList) removeNode(node *node) {
	node.prev.next = node.next
	node.next.prev = node.prev
}

func (this *LFUCache) Constructor(capacity int) {
	this.capacity = capacity
	this.cache = make(map[int]*node)
	this.frequencyMap = make(map[int]*doublyLinkedList)
	this.minimumFrequency = 0
}

func (this *LFUCache) Get(key int) int {
	node, ok := this.cache[key]
	if !ok {
		return -1
	}

	this.updateFrequency(node)
	return node.value
}

func (this *LFUCache) Put(key int, value int) {
	if this.capacity <= 0 {
		return
	}

	node, ok := this.cache[key]
	if ok {
		node.value = value
		this.updateFrequency(node)
		return
	}

	if len(this.cache) == this.capacity {
		// Remove the least frequently used item.
		dll := this.frequencyMap[this.minimumFrequency]
		delete(this.cache, dll.tail.prev.key)
		dll.removeNode(dll.tail.prev)
		if dll.head.next == dll.tail {
			delete(this.frequencyMap, this.minimumFrequency)
		}
	}

	newNode := &node{key: key, value: value, frequency: 1}
	this.cache[key] = newNode

	// Initialize freq of 1 if it doesn't exist.
	_, ok = this.frequencyMap[1]
	if !ok {
		this.frequencyMap[1] = newDoublyLinkedList()
	}

	this.frequencyMap[1].addNode(newNode)
	this.minimumFrequency = 1
}

func (this *LFUCache) updateFrequency(node *node) {
	oldFrequency := node.frequency
	dll := this.frequencyMap[oldFrequency]
	dll.removeNode(node)
	if dll.head.next == dll.tail {
		delete(this.frequencyMap, oldFrequency)

		// Update minimum frequency if needed.
		if this.minimumFrequency == oldFrequency {
			this.minimumFrequency++
		}
	}

	newFrequency := oldFrequency + 1

	// Create a new list for the increased frequency if needed.
	_, ok := this.frequencyMap[newFrequency]
	if !ok {
		this.frequencyMap[newFrequency] = newDoublyLinkedList()
	}

	this.frequencyMap[newFrequency].addNode(node)
	node.frequency = newFrequency
}

use std::collections::{HashMap, LinkedList};

struct LFUCache {
    capacity: i32,
    cache: HashMap<i32, (i32, i32)>,
    frequency_map: HashMap<i32, LinkedList<i32>>,
    key_frequency: HashMap<i32, i32>,
    min_frequency: i32,
}

impl LFUCache {
    fn new(capacity: i32) -> Self {
        LFUCache {
            capacity,
            cache: HashMap::new(),
            frequency_map: HashMap::new(),
            key_frequency: HashMap::new(),
            min_frequency: 0,
        }
    }

    fn get(&mut self, key: i32) -> i32 {
        if self.capacity == 0 {
            return -1;
        }
        if !self.cache.contains_key(&key) {
            return -1;
        }

        let (value, frequency) = *self.cache.get(&key).unwrap();
        self.update_frequency(key, frequency);
        value
    }

    fn put(&mut self, key: i32, value: i32) {
        if self.capacity == 0 {
            return;
        }

        if self.cache.contains_key(&key) {
            let (old_value, frequency) = *self.cache.get(&key).unwrap();
            self.cache.insert(key, (value, frequency));
            self.update_frequency(key, frequency);
            return;
        }

        if self.cache.len() as i32 == self.capacity {
            //Evict the least frequently used item
            let least_frequent_list = self.frequency_map.get_mut(&self.min_frequency).unwrap();
            let key_to_remove = least_frequent_list.pop_front().unwrap();

            self.cache.remove(&key_to_remove);
            self.key_frequency.remove(&key_to_remove);

            if least_frequent_list.is_empty() {
                self.frequency_map.remove(&self.min_frequency);
            }
        }

        self.cache.insert(key, (value, 1));
        self.key_frequency.insert(key, 1);

        //New key has frequency 1
        self.frequency_map.entry(1).or_insert(LinkedList::new()).push_back(key);
        self.min_frequency = 1;
    }

    fn update_frequency(&mut self, key: i32, current_frequency: i32) {
        //Update frequency when get or put called
        let linked_list = self.frequency_map.get_mut(&current_frequency).unwrap();
        
        //Remove from existing frequency list.
        linked_list.retain(|&x| x != key);

        if linked_list.is_empty() {
            self.frequency_map.remove(&current_frequency);
            
            //If min frequency is empty, update min
            if self.min_frequency == current_frequency {
                self.min_frequency += 1;
            }
        }

        let new_frequency = current_frequency + 1;
        self.cache.insert(key, (*self.cache.get(&key).unwrap().0, new_frequency));
        self.key_frequency.insert(key, new_frequency);

        self.frequency_map.entry(new_frequency).or_insert(LinkedList::new()).push_back(key);
    }
}

class LFUCache(private val capacity: Int) {

    private val cache = mutableMapOf<Int, Node>()
    private val frequencyMap = mutableMapOf<Int, DoublyLinkedList>()
    private var minimumFrequency = 0

    data class Node(val key: Int, var value: Int, var frequency: Int = 1,
                    var prev: Node? = null, var next: Node? = null)

    class DoublyLinkedList {
        var head: Node? = null
        var tail: Node? = null
        var size = 0

        fun addNodeToHead(node: Node) {
            node.next = head
            node.prev = null
            if (head != null) {
                head?.prev = node
            }
            head = node
            if (tail == null) {
                tail = node
            }
            size++
        }

        fun removeNode(node: Node) {
            val previousNode = node.prev
            val nextNode = node.next

            if (previousNode != null) {
                previousNode.next = nextNode
            } else {
                head = nextNode
            }

            if (nextNode != null) {
                nextNode.prev = previousNode
            } else {
                tail = previousNode
            }

            size--
        }
    }

    fun get(key: Int): Int {
        val node = cache[key] ?: return -1

        updateFrequency(node)

        return node.value
    }

    fun put(key: Int, value: Int) {
        if (capacity <= 0) return

        if (cache.containsKey(key)) {
            val node = cache[key]!!
            node.value = value
            updateFrequency(node)
        } else {
            if (cache.size == capacity) {
                // Need to evict LFU node
                removeLeastFrequentlyUsed()
            }

            val newNode = Node(key, value)
            cache[key] = newNode

            // Always add new node with frequency 1
            var frequencyList = frequencyMap[1]
            if (frequencyList == null) {
                frequencyList = DoublyLinkedList()
                frequencyMap[1] = frequencyList
            }
            frequencyList.addNodeToHead(newNode)

            minimumFrequency = 1
        }
    }

    private fun updateFrequency(node: Node) {
        val oldFrequency = node.frequency

        // Remove node from old frequency list
        frequencyMap[oldFrequency]?.removeNode(node)
        if (frequencyMap[oldFrequency]?.size == 0) {
            frequencyMap.remove(oldFrequency)
            // If this was the min frequency and it's now empty,
            // increment the min frequency
            if (oldFrequency == minimumFrequency) {
                minimumFrequency++
            }
        }

        // Increment node frequency
        node.frequency++
        val newFrequency = node.frequency
        var frequencyList = frequencyMap[newFrequency]

        if (frequencyList == null) {
            frequencyList = DoublyLinkedList()
            frequencyMap[newFrequency] = frequencyList
        }
        frequencyList.addNodeToHead(node)
    }

    private fun removeLeastFrequentlyUsed() {
        //Find the list with the minimum frequency
        val leastFrequentList = frequencyMap[minimumFrequency]!!
        //Remove from cache the tail of the frequency list
        val nodeToRemove = leastFrequentList.tail!!
        cache.remove(nodeToRemove.key)
        leastFrequentList.removeNode(nodeToRemove)
        //Clean up if the list is now empty
        if (leastFrequentList.size == 0) {
            frequencyMap.remove(minimumFrequency)
        }
    }
}

class LFUCache {

    private class Node {
        var key: Int
        var value: Int
        var frequency: Int

        init(key: Int, value: Int) {
            self.key = key
            self.value = value
            self.frequency = 1
        }
    }

    private var capacity: Int
    private var cache: [Int: Node]
    private var frequencyMap: [Int: DoublyLinkedList]
    private var minimumFrequency: Int

    init(_ capacity: Int) {
        self.capacity = capacity
        self.cache = [:]
        self.frequencyMap = [:]
        self.minimumFrequency = 1
    }

    func get(_ key: Int) -> Int {
        guard let node = cache[key] else { return -1 }

        updateFrequency(node)
        return node.value
    }

    func put(_ key: Int, _ value: Int) {
        guard capacity > 0 else { return }

        if let node = cache[key] {
            node.value = value
            updateFrequency(node)
            return
        }

        if cache.count == capacity {
            // Remove the least frequently used item if full
            removeLeastFrequent()
        }

        let newNode = Node(key: key, value: value)
        cache[key] = newNode

        // Add the new node to the frequency list for frequency 1
        if frequencyMap[1] == nil {
            frequencyMap[1] = DoublyLinkedList()
        }
        frequencyMap[1]!.append(newNode)
        minimumFrequency = 1
    }

    private func updateFrequency(_ node: Node) {
        let oldFrequency = node.frequency
        node.frequency += 1

        // Remove the node from its old frequency list
        frequencyMap[oldFrequency]!.remove(node)

        //If list becomes empty and it is the min frequency then increment min frequency.
        if frequencyMap[oldFrequency]!.isEmpty {
            if oldFrequency == minimumFrequency {
                minimumFrequency += 1
            }
            frequencyMap.removeValue(forKey: oldFrequency)
        }

        // Add the node to its new frequency list
        let newFrequency = node.frequency
        if frequencyMap[newFrequency] == nil {
            frequencyMap[newFrequency] = DoublyLinkedList()
        }
        frequencyMap[newFrequency]!.append(node)
    }

    private func removeLeastFrequent() {
        //This checks if the frequency map contains the minimum frequency.
        guard let leastFrequentList = frequencyMap[minimumFrequency] else { return }
        let nodeToRemove = leastFrequentList.removeFirst()
        cache.removeValue(forKey: nodeToRemove.key)

        if leastFrequentList.isEmpty {
            frequencyMap.removeValue(forKey: minimumFrequency)
        }
    }

    private class DoublyLinkedList {
        private class LinkedListNode {
            var node: Node
            var prev: LinkedListNode?
            var next: LinkedListNode?

            init(node: Node) {
                self.node = node
            }
        }

        private var head: LinkedListNode?
        private var tail: LinkedListNode?

        var isEmpty: Bool {
            return head == nil
        }

        func append(_ node: Node) {
            let newNode = LinkedListNode(node: node)

            if head == nil {
                head = newNode
                tail = newNode
            } else {
                tail!.next = newNode
                newNode.prev = tail
                tail = newNode
            }
        }

        func remove(_ node: Node) {
            var current = head
            while current != nil {
                if current!.node === node {
                    if let prevNode = current!.prev {
                        prevNode.next = current!.next
                    } else {
                        head = current!.next
                    }

                    if let nextNode = current!.next {
                        nextNode.prev = current!.prev
                    } else {
                        tail = current!.prev
                    }

                    //The node has been found and removed, no need to continue searching.
                    break
                }
                current = current!.next
            }
        }

        func removeFirst() -> Node {
			// Remove the head node, adjusting head and tail as needed.
            let nodeToRemove = head!.node
            if head === tail {
                head = nil
                tail = nil
            } else {
                head = head!.next
                head!.prev = nil
            }
            return nodeToRemove
        }
    }
}

class LFUCache

def initialize(capacity)
	@capacity = capacity
	@cache = {}
	@frequency_map = Hash.new { |h, k| h[k] = [] }
	@min_frequency = 1
end

def get(key)
	if @cache.key?(key)
		value, frequency = @cache[key]
		update_frequency(key, frequency)
		return value
	end
	-1
end

def put(key, value)
	return if @capacity <= 0

	if @cache.key?(key)
		_, frequency = @cache[key]
		@cache[key] = [value, frequency]
		update_frequency(key, frequency)
		return
	end

	if @cache.size >= @capacity
		evict_least_frequent
	end

	@cache[key] = [value, 1]
	@frequency_map[1] << key
	@min_frequency = 1 # New item has frequency 1
end

private

def update_frequency(key, current_frequency)
	@frequency_map[current_frequency].delete(key)

	# Clean up if frequency is empty and is min frequency
	if @frequency_map[current_frequency].empty? && current_frequency == @min_frequency
		@min_frequency += 1 # Increase min_frequency since current is empty
		end

	new_frequency = current_frequency + 1
	@cache[key] = [@cache[key][0], new_frequency]
	@frequency_map[new_frequency] << key
end

def evict_least_frequent
	# Need to remove the least frequent used element
	while @frequency_map[@min_frequency].empty?
		@min_frequency += 1
	end

	key_to_evict = @frequency_map[@min_frequency].shift
	@cache.delete(key_to_evict)
	# It is important to remove it from the main cache
	end
end

class LFUCache {
    private $capacity;
    private $cache = [];
    private $frequencyMap = [];
    private $keyFrequencyMap = [];
    private $minimumFrequency = 0;

    /**
     * @param Integer $capacity
     */
    function __construct($capacity) {
        $this->capacity = $capacity;
        $this->cache = [];
        $this->frequencyMap = [];
        $this->keyFrequencyMap = [];
        $this->minimumFrequency = 0;
    }

    /**
     * @param Integer $key
     * @return Integer
     */
    function get($key) {
        if (!isset($this->cache[$key])) {
            return -1;
        }

        $this->updateFrequency($key);
        return $this->cache[$key];
    }

    /**
     * @param Integer $key
     * @param Integer $value
     * @return NULL
     */
    function put($key, $value) {
        if ($this->capacity <= 0) {
            return;
        }

        if (isset($this->cache[$key])) {
            $this->cache[$key] = $value;
            $this->updateFrequency($key);
            return;
        }

        if (count($this->cache) >= $this->capacity) {
            // Evict the least frequently used item
            $this->evict();
        }

        $this->cache[$key] = $value;
        $this->keyFrequencyMap[$key] = 1;

        if (!isset($this->frequencyMap[1])) {
            $this->frequencyMap[1] = [];
        }
        array_unshift($this->frequencyMap[1], $key);

        $this->minimumFrequency = 1;
    }

    private function updateFrequency($key) {
        $frequency = $this->keyFrequencyMap[$key];

        // Remove the key from its current frequency list.
        $this->frequencyMap[$frequency] = array_values(array_filter(
            $this->frequencyMap[$frequency],
            function ($item) use ($key) {
                return $item !== $key;
            }
        ));

        if (empty($this->frequencyMap[$frequency])) {
            unset($this->frequencyMap[$frequency]);

            // If minFrequency is the frequency we just cleared, increase it.
            if ($this->minimumFrequency == $frequency) {
                $this->minimumFrequency++;
            }
        }

        $this->keyFrequencyMap[$key]++;
        $newFrequency = $frequency + 1;

        if (!isset($this->frequencyMap[$newFrequency])) {
            $this->frequencyMap[$newFrequency] = [];
        }
        array_unshift($this->frequencyMap[$newFrequency], $key);
    }

    private function evict() {
        //Find the least frequent item and remove it.
        $keysAtMinimumFrequency = $this->frequencyMap[$this->minimumFrequency];
        $keyToDelete = array_pop($keysAtMinimumFrequency);

        //Remove the key from the cache and frequency maps.
        unset($this->cache[$keyToDelete]);
        unset($this->keyFrequencyMap[$keyToDelete]);

        $this->frequencyMap[$this->minimumFrequency] = $keysAtMinimumFrequency;
        if (empty($this->frequencyMap[$this->minimumFrequency])) {
            unset($this->frequencyMap[$this->minimumFrequency]);
        }
    }
}
